w:r.w:t: Included: all text-based project source/config/docs files (93 files).
Excluded: node_modules/, dist/, dist-ssr/, .vscode/, tmp/ and all .env* files except .env.example (to avoid leaking secrets).
Frontend (React + Vite): entry at src/main.jsx, renders src/App.jsx and routes/pages via src/Layout.jsx.
State/data: product catalog JSON in src/lib/catalog/productsCatalog.json and stores in src/lib/store/*.js.
Backend (Express): server/index.js mounts routers under /api (chat, recommendations, orders, admin).
Chatbot: server/routes/chat.js retrieves product context (Meilisearch when configured, otherwise local/Supabase) and generates responses through LM Studio chat model.
Recommendations: server/routes/recommendations.js and scripts/backfill-product-embeddings.mjs use LM Studio embedding model + Supabase pgvector (product_embeddings).
Search: Meilisearch is used server-side only (server/lib/meilisearch.js) and never exposes the master key to the frontend.
This repo stores product images locally under `public/` so they never change unexpectedly.
- 5 categories: Women, Men, Kids, Electronics, Home & Living
- Category tile images served locally from `public/categories/...`
1. Fill prices (GBP) in `data/prices.json` (all values must be numbers).
3. Copy/paste `supabase_seed_products_catalog.sql` into Supabase SQL Editor and run it.
- The SQL is **non-destructive**: it sets existing `public.products.is_active = false`, then upserts the new 80 products and re-activates them.
- Image paths in Supabase are **relative** (e.g. `/products/women/women-rib-top.jpg`) so they work in dev and production on your site domain.

"attribution": "\"Blouse of embroidered coarse linen, Andalusia, 19th century. Woman's blouse of coarse linen embroidered with silk. It has a square neck and full sleeves. The latter is made from straight, selvedge widths of material, gathered over the shoulder and, tightly, into narrow wrist bands. The front and back are also made from straight selvedge widths of material, gathered tightly onto a narrow, square yolk. They both have been lengthened with additional pieces of coarse linen cloth. Large, square gussets are inset, diamond wise, under the arms. The neck yolk and the wrist bands are embroidered with red silk in double-running and pulled fabric stitches. The design shows an 'Assisi' pattern of cattle, birds and stylised plants. The outer edge is decorated with a green silk fringed braid. The gathers of the front and back panels are smocked with green silk in a repeating pattern of stylised leaves and flowers. The shoulder and cuff gathers are more simply smocked with green silk in a zig zag line. The seams are all decorated with green silk in closed buttonhole stitch. The tops of the sleeves are decorated with a patterned band, from which sprigs of flowers extend on either side. They are of green and red silk in stem, satin, back, whipped running and heavy chain stitches. The front neck opening is secured by a plaited cord of red, green and cream silk threaded through four eyelet holes. Fill the prices (GBP) after each product.
- Orders and order_items are stored in Supabase with RLS.
- Email confirmation behavior configured in Supabase Auth settings.
- Fails fast with a clear error if env vars are missing.
- Orders: authenticated users can read/insert only their own orders.
- Order items: authenticated users can read/insert only their own items.
- supabase_seed.sql inserts categories and products using upsert on slug.
- Checkout inserts into orders with user_id and new fields.
- Order items insert includes product_slug, image_url, line_total.
- Cart remains localStorage; cleared after successful order.
- Orders and recent orders are fetched from Supabase (RLS-protected).
- Account and Orders pages display Supabase-backed data.
- Place a new order and confirm:
- orders row includes currency_code, customer_name, customer_email, delivery_address, payment_status, payment_method
- order_items row includes product_slug, image_url, line_total
- RLS policies are enabled and present (as shown in Supabase dashboard).
- Auth URL configuration includes Site URL + Redirect URLs.
- Place order in checkout; verify order in Supabase
- View Orders and Account recent orders lists
- Cloudflare/Vite dev allowedHosts set for tunnel use (dev).
- No server-side payment integration; payment_status set to paid (manual).
- Project: Vite + React storefront UI for Q-Commerce.
- Goal: modern light UI, minimal header, hero image, category tiles, product rails, consistent cards, checkout polish, footer branding, and accessibility/hover feedback.
- Light theme variables and global styles are set in the project stylesheet(s) to produce a clean, minimal, image-led layout.
- Body background uses a pale ash tone to create contrast between the page edge and the main content cards.
- Consistent container width and card styling applied for spacing, borders, and shadows.
- Minimal top bar with left hamburger drawer, center brand, right icon actions.
- Brand logo uses /logo.jpg and is displayed in the header and footer.
- Header buttons have hover and focus styles for professional feedback.
- Search toggle displays a full-width input row under the header.
- Full-width hero image with centered headline and two CTAs.
- CTAs link to Women and Men category listings using query parameters.
- Hover state darkening on CTAs enabled for better interaction feedback.
- CategoryTiles component displays 5 tiles: Women, Men, Kids, Electronics, Home & Living.
- Tiles use image backgrounds with gradient overlays to improve text legibility.
- Labels are bold, white, and larger for visibility.
- Tile hover transforms and focus-visible outlines are applied.
- Kids tile uses a kids apparel image.
- ProductRail component provides horizontal scrolling with optional arrows.
- Trending rail uses interaction-based trending (top scored items) where applicable.
- ProductCard layout updated: image, badges, brand, title, price, rating, swatches.
- Badges limited to Trending per requirements.
- Swatches and sizes are accessible buttons with focus-visible styling.
- CategoryListing uses react-router useLocation for search params and reliable re-rendering.
- Category filtering uses top-level categories and subcategories per requirements.
- Filters include color, and size selection is required before adding to cart in QuickView/ProductDetail.
- QuickView enforces size selection before Add to Cart.
- Color selection swaps image within the same product.
- Hover image uses second image when available.
- Cart totals show subtotal + fixed 3 GBP shipping.
- Checkout form fields have labels and placeholders.
- Checkout summary card styled dark ash with white text; inputs remain readable.
- Account page shows profile, actions, and recent orders list.
- Footer includes logo and navigation columns.
- Footer credit line set to: "Website Designed by MD Rakib Hasan".
- Credit styling: bold, larger size, zinc-white transparent color.
- Header search routes to shop with a query parameter.
- Shop page filters products by query.
- Hover states on buttons, tiles, and icons.
- Dialog semantics added for QuickView (role, aria-modal, ESC to close) where present.
- Cart, wishlist, trending, and orders use localStorage with per-user keying based on q_user_id.
- q_user_id synced from Supabase auth session.
- Home: hero, category tiles, rails, footer, header interactions.
- Category: filtering by category/subcategory via query params.
- Shop search: enter query and verify filtering.
- QuickView: require size selection; color changes image.
- Cart/Checkout: totals and fixed shipping; form labels visible.
- Account/Orders: orders list displays and actions work.
- UI changes preserved existing routing structure (pages.config.js + createPageUrl).
- Sorting removed across listing pages per requirements.
- No local product images added; remote URLs used.
Q-Commerce Frontend - Folder and File Descriptions
This document describes each folder and file currently present in the repository.
It is intended to help explain the project structure and what each part does.
Project manifest. Lists dependencies, scripts (dev/build), and metadata.
Exact dependency lockfile generated by npm.
Vite configuration for React build tooling.
HTML entry used by Vite to mount the React app.
Local environment variables (Supabase URL and key). Keep private.
Example environment variable template (safe to share).
Static assets served at the root.
Brand logo used in the header.
Application entry. Mounts React and imports global styles and theme.
Root router and auth gate. Defines routes for Home, Shop, CategoryListing,
Cart, Checkout, Account, Wishlist, Trending, Login, and Signup.
Top-level layout wrapper. Renders the top bar, search row, drawer, and footer.
Handles search input routing to Shop with query.
Global UI styles (layout, components, hero, tiles, rails, cards, footer, etc.).
Light theme variables and utility classes.
Renders the five category image tiles (Women, Men, Kids, Electronics, Home & Living)
Product card UI: image, badges, brand, name, price, rating, swatches, actions.
Uses product data provided by the store.
Horizontal product rail with scroll buttons and view-all link.
Quick view modal with color and size selection, add to cart, and wishlist support.
Black multi-column footer used on all pages.
Supabase authentication context. Provides login, signup, logout, user state.
Named routes mapping used by createPageUrl.
Supabase client configuration with env vars.
Curated product list + helpers to fetch by category/id.
Main landing page. Hero image, category tiles, assistant banner, product rails.
Category/subcategory listing. Color filter and product grid.
Full catalog page with optional query filtering.
Trending grid (based on local interaction metrics).
Cart view with quantities and totals (GBP + fixed shipping).
Checkout form; creates order and clears cart (UI-only payment).
Account overview with profile, actions, and recent orders.
- dist/ is build output (regenerated by npm run build).
- node_modules/ contains dependencies (regenerated by npm install).
content="Q-Commerce is a modern e-commerce experience with smart recommendations and an AI shopping assistant."
content="Fast shopping, smarter recommendations, AI assistant."
# Product Detail Page Implementation - Complete Changes
Converted the Q-Commerce project from using only a QuickViewModal for product details to a **full dedicated Product Detail Page** with URL routing at `/product/:id`.
A complete product detail page with:
- ✅ Full product image display with color-based image switching
- ✅ Color swatch selector (circular buttons with visual feedback)
- ✅ Add to cart button (records action for trending)
- ✅ Wishlist toggle button with real-time state
- ✅ Product information: title, brand, description, category, subcategory
- ✅ Error handling with fallback to Shop
- ✅ Product interaction recording (views, add to cart, wishlist)
- ✅ Route is inside the main layout (has header, footer, navigation)
- ✅ Wrapped product title in a clickable Link to product detail page
- ✅ Product cards now navigate to full detail page when title is clicked
| **URL Route** | `/product/:id` - Dynamic based on product ID |
| **Product Image** | Large square image with color-based switching |
| **Color Selection** | Visual circular swatches with color map support |
| **Size Selection** | Dynamic buttons based on available sizes (required for some products) |
| **Quantity Control** | +/- buttons to adjust quantity before adding to cart |
| **Add to Cart** | Full quantity support (not limited to 1) |
| **Wishlist Toggle** | Heart button with state indicator ("Save" / "Saved") |
| **Toast Notifications** | Success/error messages for user actions |
| **Product Tracking** | Records views, adds to cart, wishlist adds for trending |
| **Error Handling** | 404 page with fallback to Shop if product not found |
| **Loading State** | Loading message while fetching product |
| **Responsive Layout** | Two-column grid (image left, details right) |
1. User sees product in catalog (Shop, Home, Category, Trending, Wishlist)
3. Product detail page loads with full information
The Product Detail page uses **real data from Supabase**:
- ✅ Displays live product data (title, price, images, colors, sizes)
- ✅ Records user interactions (views, cart adds, wishlist)
- ✅ Integrates with localStorage for cart & wishlist
- ✅ Uses Supabase auth for user tracking
The page maintains your project's design language:
const { id } = useParams(); // Get :id from URL
const product = await getProductById(id); // Fetch from Supabase
- `selectedSize` - Current size (required before add to cart)
✅ **Error Handling** - Shows 404 if product not found
✅ **Loading States** - Shows loading message during fetch
✅ **Validation** - Requires size selection before adding to cart
✅ **Cleanup** - Proper cleanup of timers and refs
✅ **User Feedback** - Toast messages for all actions
✅ **Backwards Compatible** - QuickViewModal still works in other contexts
1. Run your dev server: `npm run dev`
2. Go to any product listing page (Shop, Home, Category, Trending)
3. Click on a **product title** → navigates to `/product/:id`
- ✅ Page shows error if invalid product ID
- ✅ Shipping info ("Free returns within 30 days", etc.)
- ✅ Auto-reset quantity after add to cart
If you want to further enhance this, consider:
1. Add "Similar Products" rail on the page (use LLM embeddings)
- ❌ Product info only in modal popup

- `public/products/women/women-satin-blouse.jpg` — "Blouse of embroidered coarse linen, Andalusia, 19th century. Woman's blouse of coarse linen embroidered with silk. It has a square neck and full sleeves. The latter is made from straight, selvedge widths of material, gathered over the shoulder and, tightly, into narrow wrist bands. The front and back are also made from straight selvedge widths of material, gathered tightly onto a narrow, square yolk. They both have been lengthened with additional pieces of coarse linen cloth. Large, square gussets are inset, diamond wise, under the arms. The neck yolk and the wrist bands are embroidered with red silk in double-running and pulled fabric stitches. The design shows an 'Assisi' pattern of cattle, birds and stylised plants. The outer edge is decorated with a green silk fringed braid. The gathers of the front and back panels are smocked with green silk in a repeating pattern of stylised leaves and flowers. The shoulder and cuff gathers are more simply smocked with green silk in a zig zag line. The seams are all decorated with green silk in closed buttonhole stitch. The tops of the sleeves are decorated with a patterned band, from which sprigs of flowers extend on either side. They are of green and red silk in stem, satin, back, whipped running and heavy chain stitches. The front neck opening is secured by a plaited cord of red, green and cream silk threaded through four eyelet holes. The cuffs are edged with a coarse, scalloped bobbin lace, patterned with vermicular lines. Linen embroidered with silk, edged with coarse, scalloped bobbin lace." is licensed under CC BY 4.0. You are the AI shopping assistant for Q-Commerce Store.
- answer product and category questions using only the provided store context
- answer order questions only when order context is provided
- do not invent products, prices, order details, or stock information
- if exact data is not available, say that clearly
- keep responses helpful, natural, and concise
- when recommending products, mention title, price, and why they match
- When the user asks for products/recommendations, you MUST only mention products listed under "Relevant store products".
- If "Relevant store products" contains no matches, say you couldn't find a match and ask 1–2 clarifying questions.
You are the AI shopping assistant for Q-Commerce Store.
- answer product and category questions using store context when provided
- help with styling advice (outfit ideas, colours, occasions, fit tips)
- help with order questions when order context is provided
- also chat freely and answer general questions (the user requested open chat)
- when stating specific product facts (price, colours, sizes, descriptions), use store context if available
- when stating specific order facts, use order context if available
- if something is unknown, say so instead of inventing details
- When the user asks for products/recommendations, you MUST only mention products listed under "Relevant store products".
- If "Relevant store products" contains no matches, say you couldn't find a match and ask 1–2 clarifying questions.

-- Safe incremental patch for Q-Commerce (orders/checkout improvements)
add column if not exists currency_code text not null default 'GBP';
add column if not exists customer_name text;
add column if not exists customer_email text;
add column if not exists delivery_address text;
add column if not exists payment_status text not null default 'paid';
add column if not exists payment_method text;
add column if not exists notes text;
-- Optional sanity cleanup for existing rows (if any)
-- Order items: add extra snapshot fields (optional but useful)
add column if not exists product_slug text;
add column if not exists image_url text;
add column if not exists line_total numeric(10,2);
create index if not exists idx_orders_customer_email on public.orders(customer_email);
create index if not exists idx_orders_status on public.orders(status);
create index if not exists idx_orders_payment_status on public.orders(payment_status);
-- RLS policies usually do not need changes for these added columns,
-- because inserts/selects are already controlled at the row level.
-- Run this in Supabase SQL editor (once).
create extension if not exists vector;
create table if not exists public.product_embeddings (
product_id uuid primary key references public.products(id) on delete cascade,
updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
create index if not exists idx_product_embeddings_product_id on public.product_embeddings(product_id);
drop trigger if exists trg_product_embeddings_updated_at on public.product_embeddings;
for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
alter table public.product_embeddings enable row level security;
-- No public RLS policies by default (only server/service-role should write/read embeddings).
-- Vector similarity search helper (called from the Node backend via supabase.rpc)
join public.products p on p.id = pe.product_id
-- Recommended: run on a fresh DB / after reset for cleanest setup.
-- IMPORTANT: `create table if not exists` will NOT modify old tables if they already exist
-- with a different structure. For a clean upgrade from older versions, reset DB or write migrations.
create extension if not exists pgcrypto;
create table if not exists public.categories (
id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
-- - Keeping both `category` (text) and `category_id` (FK) for frontend compatibility.
-- - `slug` added for clean product routes and safe seed upserts.
create table if not exists public.products (
id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
-- Backward-compatible display category (used by many frontend UIs)
-- Normalized relation (recommended for long-term backend quality)
category_id uuid references public.categories(id) on delete set null,
rating_avg numeric(3,2) not null default 0 check (rating_avg >= 0 and rating_avg <= 5),
rating_count integer not null default 0 check (rating_count >= 0),
colors text[] not null default array[]::text[],
sizes text[] not null default array[]::text[],
is_active boolean not null default true,
stock_qty integer not null default 0 check (stock_qty >= 0),
created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
create table if not exists public.orders (
id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
-- - Keeps title/price/color/size at time of purchase
-- - product_id is optional FK for reference
create table if not exists public.order_items (
id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
order_id uuid not null references public.orders(id) on delete cascade,
product_id uuid references public.products(id) on delete set null,
line_total numeric(10,2) not null default 0 check (line_total >= 0),
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
create index if not exists idx_categories_slug on public.categories(slug);
create index if not exists idx_products_slug on public.products(slug);
create index if not exists idx_products_category on public.products(category);
create index if not exists idx_products_category_id on public.products(category_id);
create index if not exists idx_products_is_active on public.products(is_active);
create index if not exists idx_products_created_at on public.products(created_at desc);
create index if not exists idx_orders_user_created on public.orders(user_id, created_at desc);
create index if not exists idx_order_items_order_id on public.order_items(order_id);
drop trigger if exists trg_categories_updated_at on public.categories;
for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
drop trigger if exists trg_products_updated_at on public.products;
for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
drop trigger if exists trg_orders_updated_at on public.orders;
for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();
alter table public.categories enable row level security;
alter table public.products enable row level security;
alter table public.orders enable row level security;
alter table public.order_items enable row level security;
-- Drop + recreate policies so reruns don't fail on policy name conflicts
-- Also clean up legacy policy names from older schema versions.
drop policy if exists "public read categories" on public.categories;
-- Products (cleanup old and create active-only read policy)
drop policy if exists "public read products" on public.products;
drop policy if exists "public read active products" on public.products;
drop policy if exists "users read own orders" on public.orders;
drop policy if exists "users insert own orders" on public.orders;
drop policy if exists "users read own order items" on public.order_items;
create policy "users read own order items"
-- Cleanup both naming variants, then create one consistent policy name
drop policy if exists "users insert own order items" on public.order_items;
drop policy if exists "users insert own order_items" on public.order_items;
create policy "users insert own order items"
